items.briefing:
Phishing attacks are growing more sophisticated and harder to spot, with AI now being used by cybercriminals to create highly convincing fake emails and messages at scale. Staff should be especially cautious about unexpected emails asking you to click links, provide login details, or share personal information — even if they look legitimate. Separately, a Chinese cybercrime group known as TA4922 is expanding its attacks globally, meaning Australian organisations like ours could be targeted.

On a positive note, Google has started rolling out fake call detection on Android phones, which can help identify scam callers. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected call or email asking for credentials or sensitive information, always verify the request through a separate, known contact method before responding.

featured.title: Google rolls out fake call detection on Android phones
featured.quote: Google's spoofed call detection directly protects millions of Australian Android users from financial fraud.
